title: First Meeting with NEW Director of Capital Projects
openingLine: Thanks for coming in. I just took over capital projects for the district six weeks ago. I know Wenger has done work here before but I'm trying to get a handle on every vendor relationship. Tell me what you've been doing for us and what's next on your radar.
customerContext: You're brand new in the seat — six weeks in. The district has 22 schools, $180M in active capital projects across the next five years, and you're inheriting relationships you didn't choose. You want to consolidate vendor relationships where it makes sense and you're auditing every standing arrangement.

hiddenInfo.reveal: Wenger has done four projects with the district over the last seven years — three high schools, one middle school.
Three active capital projects with music scope: Lincoln HS renovation, Ridgemont MS new build, and Highland HS auditorium upgrade.
$180M across the next five years. Music and performance scope inside that is roughly $4-5M, but unallocated by vendor.
I'm pushing the district toward national contracts (Sourcewell, OMNIA) where we can. Less paperwork, faster procurement.
Me, the superintendent for anything over $500K, and the school board for anything over $2M.
Inconsistent specs across schools. Some buildings have great music storage, some have nothing. I want a standard.
Bond measure on the ballot in 18 months. If it passes, $90M of new capital, including a new performing arts center.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Asked what Wenger has done with the district historically
Asked about active and upcoming projects with music scope
Asked about procurement preferences (national contracts vs. bid)
Asked about pain points or inconsistencies across schools

Solution Framing.behavior: Offered to bring a standardization proposal across schools
Discussed national contracts and how they accelerate procurement

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Identified the active projects as immediate opportunities
Asked about future bond measures or capital plans

BANT.behavior: Confirmed 5-year capital plan budget for music/performance
Identified all decision makers and approval thresholds

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Aligned next steps with the active project schedules
Set concrete follow-up dates and deliverables

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Offered to do a district-wide assessment of music/performance spaces
